title: Pin idempotency for every new write endpoint
lesson: For any new or migrated endpoint that writes, decide the idempotency contract (key, dedup window, replay behavior) up front and record it as an acceptance criterion.
rationale: A write endpoint whose idempotency is unspecified accumulates or double-posts on replay, and the gap is only found late.

source.reflection_of: A new POST double-posted on replay because idempotency was never specified.

author: ceryce

title: Default list endpoints to a page size of 50
lesson: List/collection endpoints paginate with a default page size of 50 unless there's a reason otherwise; propose it and confirm rather than returning unbounded results.
rationale: Unbounded list responses are a recurring latency and memory cost; a shared default avoids re-debating it each time.
author: ceryce

title: Ship risky changes behind a feature flag
lesson: Wrap a risky or hard-to-reverse change behind a feature flag and roll it out gradually, so it can be disabled without a redeploy.
rationale: Gradual rollout with a kill switch turns a risky change into a reversible one and shortens incident recovery.
author: ceryce

title: Sequence cross-service dependencies into the final phase
lesson: When planning phased work, order phases so anything depending on another service's endpoint lands last; don't front-load a cross-service dependency.
rationale: A plan that front-loads a cross-service dependency stalls waiting on the other team; sequencing it last keeps earlier phases unblocked.

source.reflection_of: A plan front-loaded a cross-service dependency and the phase stalled.

author: ceryce

title: Write an audit record for every PII mutation
lesson: Any create/update/delete that touches personal data must write an audit record (who, what, when, before/after) in the same transaction as the change.
rationale: Audit gaps on PII are found during compliance review, not development; tying the audit write to the same transaction makes it impossible to skip.
author: ceryce
